nodes.name: 鹳形目
nodes.summary: 鹳形目（Ciconiiformes）是图谱中的目级分类节点，当前关联17种鸟类，如钳嘴鹳、非洲秃鹳、Greater adjutant等17种鸟类。

nodes.id: loc-约翰-弗里德里希-格梅林-8a33c9ca
nodes.name: 约翰·弗里德里希·格梅林
nodes.summary: 约翰·弗里德里希·格梅林是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联64种鸟类，包括灰穴䳍、杂色穴䳍、葡萄胸鴨等64种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 农药污染
nodes.summary: 农业化学品通过水体和食物链影响鸟类生存。

nodes.name: 潘塔納爾濕地
nodes.summary: 潘塔納爾濕地是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联22种鸟类，包括Bare-faced curassow、Maguari stork、黑冠白颈鹭等22种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 猎捕压力
nodes.summary: 直接猎捕或误捕造成额外死亡。

nodes.name: 巴拉那盆地
nodes.summary: 巴拉那盆地是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联1种鸟类，包括Maguari stork，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: Maguari stork
nodes.summary: Maguari stork（Maguari stork，学名 Ciconia maguari），分类上属于鹳形目，并归入鹳科，在图谱中标记为无危（LC）物种，已关联4个分布地点节点，常见栖息地包括湿地、稀树草原、沼泽，主要威胁包括栖息地破坏、滩涂围垦、农药污染等5项。
locations: 约翰·弗里德里希·格梅林

nodes.name: 湿地
nodes.summary: 浅水、沼泽与植被斑块组成的高生产力生态系统。

nodes.name: 彭巴草原
nodes.summary: 彭巴草原是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联10种鸟类，包括棕头草雁、Magellanic snipe、Maguari stork等10种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 人类活动扰动
nodes.summary: 旅游、施工和高频活动影响繁殖或觅食。

nodes.name: 沼泽
nodes.summary: 具有较高含水量和密生植被的浅水环境。

nodes.name: 栖息地破坏
nodes.summary: 开发、采伐和围垦等活动导致适宜生境减少。

nodes.name: 滩涂围垦
nodes.summary: 滨海滩涂被转化为建设或农业用地。

nodes.name: 鹳科
nodes.summary: 鹳科（Ciconiidae）是图谱中的科级分类节点，当前关联17种鸟类，如钳嘴鹳、非洲秃鹳、Greater adjutant等17种鸟类。

nodes.name: 稀树草原
nodes.summary: 东非常见的开放草地与湿地边缘复合景观。

nodes.name: LC
nodes.summary: LC表示 IUCN 无危等级，当前关联8208种鸟类，如鸵鸟、鸸鹋、南方鶴鴕等8208种鸟类。

links.target: loc-约翰-弗里德里希-格梅林-8a33c9ca
links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The maguari stork (Ciconia maguari) is a large species of stork that inhabits seasonal wetlands over much of South America, and is very similar in appearance to the white stork; albeit slightly larger.King CE

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: Colonial Waterbirds 10: 181-202. and the Pampas of Argentina. It occurs seasonally and is common in the Pantanal of Argentina. Large numbers migrate to the Pantanal in the wet season, probably from the Parana Basin and Rio Grand do Sul.Antas PTZ

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: Colonial Waterbirds 10: 181-202. and the Pampas of Argentina. It occurs seasonally and is common in the Pantanal of Argentina. Large numbers migrate to the Pantanal in the wet season, probably from the Parana Basin and Rio Grand do Sul.Antas PTZ

links.label: 栖息于
links.evidence: The maguari stork (Ciconia maguari) is a large species of stork that inhabits seasonal wetlands over much of South America, and is very similar in appearance to the white stork; albeit slightly larger.King CE

links.label: 栖息于
links.evidence: Its habitat largely comprises open lowland and shallow-water wetland such as tropical wet savannah grasslands, marshes, mudflats, and flooded fields. It more occasionally frequents dry fields, but invariably avoids forested regions. Numerous maguari stork assemblages have been observed in their habitat during the dry season, where they forage in low-level bodies of water where prey is concentrated. The maguari stork lives in sympatry with jabiru and wood stork where the ranges of these three species overlap, especially in the Venezuelan llanos. Of all American stork species, the maguari stork has the smallest geographical range

links.label: 栖息于
links.evidence: Its habitat largely comprises open lowland and shallow-water wetland such as tropical wet savannah grasslands, marshes, mudflats, and flooded fields. It more occasionally frequents dry fields, but invariably avoids forested regions. Numerous maguari stork assemblages have been observed in their habitat during the dry season, where they forage in low-level bodies of water where prey is concentrated. The maguari stork lives in sympatry with jabiru and wood stork where the ranges of these three species overlap, especially in the Venezuelan llanos. Of all American stork species, the maguari stork has the smallest geographical range

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: The primary threats to this species are anthropogenic habitat disturbance and hunting for food. A common human disturbance occurs through habitat destruction via land reclamation from marshes for agriculture, which has occurred especially in southeast Brazil and may therefore evoke conservation concern for the species in this area. Claiming land for agriculture by digging canals, together with land filling and sewage discharges may also threaten dry-season foraging environments for maguari storks especially on the northern Rio de Janeiro coast. The maguari stork is vulnerable to nesting habitat destruction because it shows nest-site fidelity, and will continue to nest in the same place even after onset of recent anthropogenic disturbance. Use of pesticides may also adversely affect the health and breeding success of this species. The capture of individuals for food presents another survival threat and occurs particularly in the Southern AmazonSick H
